question.label: Practice commitment
question.name: What one habit do you want to practice before we meet again?
question.description: Closes on a concrete near-term behavior change with ownership.

answer: I will start by writing one explicit priority rule I will use when time pressure hits this month.

assessment.note: He named a specific self-directed practice for handling time pressure, which is a concrete positive commitment on growth and clarity.

history.answer_excerpt: I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.
Feedback has helped, though I still miss consistency under time pressure.
I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.
Feedback has helped, though I still miss consistency under time pressure.
I sometimes focus on polish and lose sight of the core message.
I want clearer examples of what excellent junior-level work looks like here.
When reviews are broad, I am not sure what to change first.
I would like to grow faster in concept rationale, not just execution speed.
A tighter loop on one skill at a time would help me improve.
If we align on concrete criteria, I can work against them confidently.
I will start by writing one explicit priority rule I will use when time pressure hits this month.
